        capability. This is not a good practice and hope to optimize it.

Check if nodes in cluster mounted the filesystem.
        :param node_ip: specify the node by a node ip, or check all nodes
                        in cluster, ususally used with uuid together.
        :returns: return True if mount on cluster node, False for else, raise
                  exception if connect remote node fail.

Compare local cluster configuration with the specified node.
        This method is specifically designed to support multithreading and
        share the results via a shared dictionary with fixed keys, which is
        thread safe.
        :param node_id: Specify a node by the node number.

z-Determine the uuid from the content metadata.Z

Get uuid from %s failed, %sN)	r
Ocfs2Cluster._get_uuidc
}	t	j
 Try to check the residual heartbeat region.
        It could not eliminate the residual heartbeat completely, so adding
        this check.
        Another process refers to the mount point by cloning a new mount
        namespace, leading to the s_active.count in struct super_block of VFS
        could not be decreased to zero, and umount returns exit code zero from
        VFS, but ocfs2_dismount_volume actually not be executed, then there is
        a residual heartbeat region.
        r?
z*/sys/kernel/config/cluster/%s/heartbeat/%sz
/sys/kernel/debug/ocfs2/%sz
ocfs2_hb_ctl -K -u %sTF)
z2ocfs2_hb_ctl remove %s heartbeat fail, possible %sz
Found residual heartbeat on %s, detailed info can be found via "for file in $(find /proc -name "mounts"); do echo $file; grep pool_name $file; done"z%There is a residual heartbeat region.z"pgrep -l "fsck.ocfs2|tunefs.ocfs2"z%It is possible %s started a heartbeatN)

z?Provide various operations on ocfs2 cluster configuration file.c
Even if we don't have ocfs2 cluster.conf file at hand while
        instantiate this class, we may still use this class to generate the
        configuration file and configure the whole ocfs2 cluster. So let it
        znOcfs2 cluster configuration file is not existed! Do not forget to configure this cluster later if you have to.N)

get_node_name
Ocfs2ClusterConf.get_node_namec
The cluster nodes is a list, a structure example:
        [
            {
                'ip_port': '7100',
                'cluster': 'host_pool',
                'ip_address': '10.125.33.89',
                'number': '2',
                'name': 'cvknode89'
            },
            {
                'ip_port': '7100',
                'cluster': 'host_pool',
                'ip_address': '10.125.33.90',
                'number': '1',
                'name': 'cvknode90'
            }
        ]
        z"Can not find ocfs2 cluster config.r

Return a dictionary contains all parameter and value.
        example:
        {
            'o2cb_heartbeat_threshold': '61',
            'o2cb_reconnect_delay_ms': '2000',

            'o2cb_bootcluster': 'host_pool',
            'o2cb_fence_method': 'umount'
